,, .l
DOMTEY TAXPAYERS UNTTED
Post Office Box 39293
Dorney, CA 90239
VlA TELECOPIER
May 24, L992
llark Morodomi, Esq.
Fair PoLitical Practices Comrnission
428 ttJtt Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, california 
9 5814
Re: DOWNEY TAXPAYERS UNITED
I. D. No. 92]-]-23
Dear !lr. Morodomi:
on the tetephone today, I advj-sed you that I had drafted a letter
to FPPC regarding certain questions which I had concerning late
expenditures and late contribution reports. Enclosed is a copy of
that letter dated llay 27, 1992 which has not been previously
mailed.
Al-so, on the telephone today, t specifically asked you reqarding
the requirements for filing 
Form 496 given the circumstances of our
situation. 
The questions which I asked you earlier by telephone
today reLate to Issue Number 2 in the enclosed letter.
I am sending now, by separate cover, the letter dated May 27, 1992,
to the FPPC. I am enclosing this telecopy for your information
only with regards to my oral request. I am still- very interested
in hearing from you by telephone as to the requirements for filing
Form 496 by our committee.
Very truly yours,
& LUMSDATNE
JAL: bda.21
Lumsda i ne


DOW}IEY TAXPAYERS UNITED
Post Office Box 39293
DoYney, CA 90239
Itay 27 , 1992
Fair Poli-ticaI Practices commission
428 ttJtt Street, suite 600
sacramento, california 
9 5814
Re3 DOIINEY TAXPAYERS UNTTED
I . D. No . 92L1-23
Dear Madam or Sir:
As chairman of this political action cornrnittee, I am confused as to
the filing requirements as required by ca]ifornia Government code
sections 82031-, A2O25, 82036 and 82036.5. These sections pertain
prinari.ly to Form Numbers 496 and 497 relating to late, independent
expenditures and late contribution reports. As an attorney, I have
reviewed those code sections, as well as the forms, but still 
have
several- questions which are set forth below, after a brief discussion
of the facts.
r. 
THE FACTS
Downey Taxpayers United (DTU) was formed to oppose efforts by
the city Enployees Coalition (cEc) to hand-pick and endorse certain
candidates. At the forming of DTU, the cEc had not yet endorsed any
specific candidates. After the CEC endorsed certaj,n specific candi-
dates, DTU decided to endorse certain other candidates. Up to this
point, DTU has raised only $300 in contributions.
DTU decided to put out a maiter setting forth their position
and, in the same rnai-ler, supported and opposed certain candidates.
we contacted a prlnter known to me, who stated that the approxirnate
cost would be $6,600 for printing and nailing of the maifers. on I'Iay
2L, L992, the printer apparently deposited the rnailers with the post
office. 
on saturday, NIay 23, ]-992, constituents in the city of
Downey began receiving copies of the mailers. f did not discover
this until my return from out of town for the holiday, Tuesday, May
26, L992.
Four different types of mailers were rnailed to each of the four
districts of the city. 
Each mailer contained an identical statement
of positj.on of DTU. In one district, 
the mailer supported one city-
wide candidate and opposed one city-wide candidate. fn the other


Fair Political Practices Commission
NIay 27, 1992
Page Two
three districts, 
the mailer supported the same city-wide
and opposed the same city-wide candidate and additionally
one district 
candidate and opposed at least one district
(In one district, 
two opposition candidates were opposed)
cand idate
supported
candidate.
Issue Number 1:
Is this a rrslate mailerrr as
referenced in Government code section 82o4a.3?
covernment code section 82048.: specifies a trmass mailing which
supports or opposes a total, of four or more candidatesr'. rrMass
rnailing'r is defined in Governrnent Code section a2o4l.5 to mean
"substantially similar pieces of mail". 
Since each mailer for each
district 
supports, at most, two candidates and opposes, at most,
three candidates, my interpretation is that the mailers are not
'rslate mailers'r within the rneaning of covernment code section
82048.3. otherwise, that code section should refer to a nailer which
'rsupports q45! opposes a totaf of four or more candidatesrr.
Issue Number 2: 
fs a Forn 496 required to be
filed at this time?
we have not yet been invoiced by the printer, although we have
been advised that the amount is probably about $6,600 for a1l four
districts.
Governrnent Code section 82036.5 defines a rrLate independent
expenditurert as one which I'totals in the aggregate one Thousand
Dollars ($1,000) or more and is made for or against any specific
candidatett. with a cornbined cost of approximately $0,600, the cost
of the nail,er for each of the four districts would be approximately
$1,650. since each mailer supports at least one candidate and
opposes at least one candidate, I would interpret Government sectj.on
a2o36.5 as not reguiring a filing of Form 496 because the expenditure
for or against each specific candidate erould be, at most, one-half of
$1,650, or $825 for each candidate supported or opposed.
Further, Governnent Code section 82025 defines expenditure as
rran enforceable promise to make pa)anent" and further, specifies the
date of payment as srhen |tpa)rment is made or the date consideration,
if any, is receivedrt. Since our cornmittee requested work to be done,
arguably, there is an enforceable pronise to make paynent. However,
as to the date of the expenditure, I would understand the require-
ments of these code sections as not coming into pLay until an invoice
is actually received from the printer. 
Otherwise, r,/e lrould have to
guess as to the dollar amount to be stated for each candidate on Form
496.


Falr Political Practices commission
May 27 | 1992
Page Three
Issue Number 3: 
Is Form 497 relating to late
contributions required to be filed at this time?
At this tirne, less than $300 has been raised by DTU. we expect
to raise contributions to cover the cost of the nailers or, in the
alternative, if the j,nvoice has been paid before all such contribu-
tions are raised, nyself and certain other members of the loosely-
formed steering commj.ttee for this group would loan the money to DTU
to make payment on the invoice. However, no such loans have been
made at this tine.
A further .issue is that Government code section 82036 specifi-
cally refers to contributions of $L,o0o, or more, received by rra
candidate, a controlled committee, or cornrnittee formed or existing
prirnarily to support or oppose a candidate". DTU does not appear to
fall within any of those categories. Accordingly, my conclusion is
that Form 497 is not required to be fi1ed, certainly not at this time
because of the lack of contributions or loans. Indeed, I would
conclude that the committee is not reguired to file a Fom 497 at any
time since DTU is not rta candidate, a controlled comrnittee, or a
cornrnittee forrned or existing primarily to support or oppose a candi-
daterr .
II. 
SUMMARY
Rather than risk being in violation of any of the code sections, I am
asking for a definitive opinion fron the FPPC and confirm the follow-
ing interpretations of the above-referenced code sections:
1. 
Forn 496 need not be filed because there has been no
expenditure exceeding $1,000 for or against any specific
candidate, since, at most, DTU expended $825 for each
candidate.
2. 
If Form 496 is to be filed because of some other
interpretation of Government Code section 82036.5, Form 496
is not required to be filed untif after receipt of an
invoice from the printer.
3. 
Because each mailer supports, at most, two candidates
and opposes, at most, two candidates, the mailers are not a
rrsl,ate nailerrt .
4. 
Form 497 is not required to be filed because DTU is
not a rrcandidate, controLled committee or cornrnittee forned
or existing prinarily to support or oppose a candidatetl
within the meaning of covernment code section 82036.


Fair PoIitical Practices commission
ylay 27, 1992
Page Four
on behalf of DTU, and the voters of the city of Downey, I thank you
for your consideration of this matter.
Very truly yours,
jal/bdaldtv.22

1.

3.

rrsl,ate nailerrt .
4. 
Form 497 is not required to be filed because DTU is

within the meaning of covernment code section 82036.
